hot_topics.topic: Trump Administration's New Tariffs and Trade Policy
hot_topics.synthesis: The Trump administration has enacted a new wave of tariffs on numerous countries, replacing previous global duties and drawing criticism from U.S. allies over forced labor claims. These policies are contributing to public frustration over gas prices, which many Americans believe have risen more sharply than they actually have, potentially impacting midterm elections. Rivian has even sued the government for a full refund of Trump tariffs, highlighting the economic consequences.

hot_topics.topic: Escalating US-Iran Conflict and Middle East Tensions
hot_topics.synthesis: Tensions in the Middle East are escalating as the US and Iran exchange strikes in the Gulf, with President Trump considering a major escalation in the ongoing conflict. Houthi attacks on Saudi tankers have caused oil prices to surge, raising fears of wider regional conflict and global economic damage. The US government also warns of Iran-linked hackers disrupting American water and energy providers, while new deals in Iraq aim to bypass the Strait of Hormuz.

hot_topics.topic: Tesla's Electronic Door Handles Prompt Safety Rule Changes
hot_topics.synthesis: Tesla's electronic door handles, which have been linked to several deaths, are prompting the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) to consider tougher safety rules for the entire auto industry. Instead of a defect investigation into Tesla specifically, NHTSA is pursuing broader rulemaking that would mandate a robust and obvious door egress system in all new motor vehicles.

items.title: Signal then storm
items.summary: For 50 years, researcher Allan Basbaum has been dedicated to finding the origins of chronic pain within the nervous system, with the ultimate goal of identifying points where pain can be stopped. His research seeks to unravel the fundamental mechanisms of pain.

items.title: A woman got a UTI. Two years later, the bacteria had evolved, invaded her brain.
items.summary: A 63-year-old woman lost vision due to a UTI bacterium that evolved to invade her brain, forming abscesses. This remarkable case, reported in the New England Journal of Medicine, highlights how infections can dangerously evolve within a patient.

items.title: AI firms want more data centers; Trump's EPA may give neighbors less say
items.summary: The Trump administration is considering an EPA rule change that could reduce public input on permitting new polluting facilities, including those for data centers. This potential rollback, facing community pushback, aims to streamline development but raises concerns about local air pollution.

items.title: Google hit with $1 billion in fines as EU braces for Trump battle
items.summary: Google has been fined over $1 billion by the European Commission for Digital Markets Act violations, specifically for self-preferencing its own services in search and restricting app developers from directing users to cheaper external options. The company must make changes within 60 days to avoid further daily penalties.

items.title: Sony's decision to ditch discs was practically inevitable, data shows
items.summary: Sony's decision to discontinue physical game disc sales by 2028 is supported by years of declining physical sales data, with units plummeting from 297 million in 2009 to 37 million recently. This trend also shows a drastic reduction in PlayStation titles selling over 100,000 physical units.

items.title: CJP protest: The youth movement India's Modi didn't see coming
items.summary: India is experiencing one of its largest student movements in years, known as the CJP protest, which originated from exam paper leaks and poses a significant challenge for Prime Minister Modi's government. Thousands have taken to the streets to demonstrate their concerns.

items.title: Ex-governor convicted of murdering a pregnant student in Kenya
items.summary: A former governor in Kenya has been convicted of murdering a pregnant student with whom he had an affair. The verdict in this high-profile case brings justice for the victim.

items.title: Families still search for bodies a month after Venezuela earthquakes
items.summary: Families in Venezuela are still searching for bodies a month after devastating earthquakes, with the official death toll surpassing 5,300 and estimates for missing persons reaching up to 50,000. The country continues to grapple with a profound humanitarian crisis and recovery efforts.

items.title: Four Palestinians and two Israelis killed in West Bank shooting
items.summary: Six people, including four Palestinians and two Israelis, were killed in a shooting incident in the West Bank amidst conflicting accounts from both sides. Palestinians reported settler attacks, while the Israeli military claimed a Palestinian stole a weapon and opened fire.

items.title: Google and Tesla shares plunge as AI spending rattles markets
items.summary: Shares of Google and Tesla plunged as investors expressed concerns about the financial benefits and timelines for return on investment from major tech companies' extensive AI spending. The market is questioning the immediate profitability of the AI race.

items.title: Houthi attacks raise fears of wider Middle East conflict and more global economic damage
items.summary: The Iran-backed group in Yemen has threatened to target Saudi vessels in a vital shipping route.

items.title: India's 'cockroach' protesters hold talks with government as stalemate continues
items.summary: The meeting came hours after activist Sonam Wangchuk announced he was ending his 26-day hunger strike.

items.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
items.summary: At least 12 Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore, raising serious questions about their welfare and legal fate. Authorities are investigating the circumstances surrounding the human trafficking operation.

items.title: Nicaragua's legislature debates barring opposition from elections
items.summary: Nicaragua's legislature is debating a bill that would bar opposition parties from participating in elections, following President Daniel Ortega's call for a 'blockade' against them. Critics fear this move could further undermine democracy in the country.

items.title: South Africa's president wins bid to temporarily halt impeachment inquiry over 'Farmgate'
items.summary: South Africa's president has won a temporary halt to an impeachment inquiry related to the 'Farmgate' scandal, involving the theft of large sums of cash from his private farm. The ruling offers a brief reprieve from the ongoing scrutiny.

items.title: Tanzanian lecturer arrested over anti-government protests charged with terrorism
items.summary: A Tanzanian lecturer, arrested during anti-government protests advocating for democratic reforms, has been charged with terrorism. He is among more than 130 individuals detained in connection with the planned demonstrations.

items.title: Tech titan ordered to pay ex-wife $644m in divorce settlement
items.summary: A tech titan, who chairs one of South Korea's largest companies, has been ordered to pay his ex-wife $644 million in a high-profile divorce settlement. The ruling concludes a widely followed legal battle that captivated the nation.

items.title: Teen jailed for exam cheating in Guinea dies after sickle cell complications, father says
items.summary: A 17-year-old teen jailed for exam cheating in Guinea died from sickle cell complications, according to his father. The case has raised concerns about the justice system and the treatment of minors in detention.

items.title: Tens of thousands evacuated from French tourist spot and near Madrid as wildfires spread
items.summary: Tens of thousands of people have been evacuated from a French tourist spot and areas near Madrid as widespread wildfires continue to rage. Officials have ordered evacuations, highlighting the intensifying environmental crisis in Europe.

items.title: Ukraine's ousted defence minister insists on being reinstated
items.summary: Ukraine's ousted defense minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, is publicly demanding reinstatement and has refused President Zelensky's offer of an alternative post. This disagreement indicates an ongoing power struggle within Ukraine's leadership.

items.title: US and Iran trade more strikes in Gulf as fears of escalation mount
items.summary: It comes as Iran condemned the US plan to use frozen Iranian assets to pay for war damages.

items.title: US hits dozens of countries with new wave of tariffs
items.summary: Imposed citing forced labour concerns, the levies replace a temporary global duty brought in after the US Supreme Court struck down tariffs in February.

items.title: Watch: Another day, more Trump tariffs - but are they working?
items.summary: The BBC's Samira Hussain explains how President Trump's tariffs affect US economy, who pays the price, and whether they're working.

items.title: Watch: Driver dressed as Spider-Man whisks man in a wheelchair across busy road
items.summary: Traffic junction footage captured a heartwarming moment of a driver dressed as Spider-Man assisting a man in a wheelchair across a busy road. The video shows the unexpected act of kindness in a public setting.

items.title: Watch: Wildfires rage across Spain and France
items.summary: Wildfires continue to rage across Spain and France, prompting Spain's government to declare a national emergency and French authorities to order evacuations in popular tourist areas. The widespread fires are causing significant disruption and environmental damage.

items.title: Why have thousands taken to the streets? India's 'cockroach' protest explained
items.summary: India's 'cockroach' protest, a large youth movement, originated from exam paper leaks and has become one of the country's most significant demonstrations in recent years. The article explains why thousands have taken to the streets and the symbolic use of a cockroach mascot.

items.title: Claude Cookbook
items.summary: The Claude Cookbook provides a collection of recipes and guidelines for effectively utilizing the Claude AI platform. It serves as a resource for developers and users looking to implement Claude's various capabilities.

items.title: Flux 3
items.summary: Flux 3 represents a new development in video-action models, designed to enhance AI's ability to interpret and create dynamic visual content. This release builds upon previous iterations of the Flux technology.

items.title: Flux 3 X Mimic: The Next Generation of Video-Action Models
items.summary: Flux 3 X Mimic is presented as the next generation of video-action models, aiming to advance AI capabilities in understanding and generating dynamic video content. The article details new developments in this technology.

items.title: I regret migrating to Codeberg
items.summary: An article details a user's regret over migrating to Codeberg, describing challenges and disappointments encountered with the platform. It provides a personal account of issues experienced after switching git hosting services.

items.title: It's getting harder to focus every day
items.summary: An article explores the increasing difficulty people face in maintaining focus daily, examining the challenges and potential underlying reasons for declining attention spans in modern society. It suggests various factors contributing to this trend.

items.title: Nothing works and everyone is euphoric
items.summary: An opinion piece argues that a pervasive sense of euphoria exists despite widespread societal dysfunction, suggesting a disconnect between reality and public perception. The author explores how this phenomenon impacts modern life.

items.title: Show HN: Echo – Fable-level results at 1/3 the cost using open-weight models
items.summary: Echo is an AI system that combines multiple open-weight models to achieve Fable-level results at one-third of the cost. The system dynamically allocates computation and combines model outputs for optimal performance, often outperforming individual models.

items.title: Writing by hand is good for your brain
items.summary: Writing by hand offers cognitive benefits, enhancing brain function and learning by engaging different neural pathways compared to typing. Research suggests the physical act of handwriting can improve overall brain health.

items.title: A defiant Trump imposes replacement tariffs on biggest U.S. trading partners
items.summary:
The administration timed a new set of tariffs on all of its largest trading partners to take effect at the same time the old, global tariff ended.
(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)

items.title: A former nun explains why she ran away from her 'Cloistered' life
items.summary: A former nun recounts her experience of leaving a cloistered Carmelite monastery after 12 years, detailing her life of prayer and obedience in her memoir, 'Cloistered: My Years as a Nun.' She shares insights into her decision to run away.

items.title: Morning news brief
items.summary: U.S.-Iran strikes escalate, affecting global energy markets, Trump imposes double-digit tariffs on dozens of countries, Vigil held in Madison, Wisconsin, for man killed by police.

items.title: Netflix viewers the world over are binge-watching South African series 'The Polygamist.' Here's why
items.summary: Netflix's South African series 'The Polygamist,' a Zulu-language drama combining sex and scandal with a portrait of modern South Africa, is gaining global viewership. Its international popularity highlights the appeal of diverse storytelling.

items.title: Oil surges to $100 per barrel. And, Trump imposes a new round of tariffs
items.summary:
Oil surged to $100 per barrel after Iran-backed Houthi rebels attacked two Saudi tankers in the Red Sea. And, President Trump overnight replaced expiring tariffs with a slew of new ones.
(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)

items.title: Trump threatens a major escalation in Iran as the war nears the end of Month 5
items.summary:
The U.S. continued airstrikes in Iran while Tehran launched retaliatory attacks on U.S. targets in the Gulf, as President Trump said he was close to deciding whether to launch "a massive attack."
(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)

items.title: U.S. allies hit with new tariffs object to Trump's forced labor claims
items.summary:
Australia and the European Union were among U.S. trade partners who criticized the tariffs.
(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)

items.title: West Virginia paid digital nomads to move there during COVID. Many are staying
items.summary: West Virginia's 'Ascend West Virginia' program, which offered cash and incentives for digital nomads to relocate during COVID, has successfully encouraged many participants to stay. This initiative aims to boost the state's economy by attracting remote workers.

items.title: An opinionated guide to which AI to use to do stuff
items.summary: A new guide highlights the significant advancements in AI, particularly with agentic systems that use AI models with tools to perform complex tasks, moving beyond simple chatbots. For high-stakes situations, advanced models like Claude Opus/Fable or ChatGPT's GPT-5.6 Sol are recommended due to their lower error rates.

items.title: Poll: Republicans’ gas price problem is even worse than it seems
items.summary:
Gas prices are high. Americans think they’re even higher.
Three in five Americans believe gas prices in their area have risen by more than they actually have, according to an analysis of new results from The POLITICO Poll. It’s those perceptions that candidates will be running ag…

items.title: Rob Bonta on blocking the Paramount merger, CNN concerns and Trump’s DOJ
items.summary: Rob Bonta discusses his perspectives on various political and legal issues, including his stance on blocking the Paramount merger, concerns regarding CNN, and views on Trump's Department of Justice. The video offers insights into his positions on these topics.

items.title: AMD takes on Nvidia with its Helios AI rack-scale system
items.summary: AMD is challenging Nvidia in the AI hardware market with its new Helios AI rack-scale system, slated to ship to customers later this year. This system aims to provide a competitive solution for advanced AI infrastructure.

items.title: Anthropic updates Claude voice mode with more capable models
items.summary: Claude's new voice model will let you reschedule your meeting or draft an email.

items.title: Bluesky’s AI assistant Attie expands into an open social research tool
items.summary: Bluesky’s AI assistant, Attie, is expanding its capabilities to become an open social research tool, allowing users to ask questions about news, trends, and conversations across Bluesky and other AT Protocol-based applications. This enhancement aims to facilitate broader social data analysis.

items.title: Facebook launches a dedicated Marketplace app for sellers, adds a free verification system
items.summary: Facebook has launched a dedicated Marketplace app for frequent sellers and introduced a free verification system to streamline transactions and build trust. The new 'Seller' app aims to improve the experience for individuals listing items for sale.

items.title: Insurance startup Corgi reportedly raised more money at $4B — its third round in 8 weeks
items.summary: Insurance startup Corgi has reportedly raised additional funding at a $4 billion valuation, marking its third successful round in eight weeks amid an AI-driven investment boom. This rapid succession of high-value funding rounds highlights intense interest in AI startups.

items.title: Meta drops out of a major clean energy pact as its natural gas buildout accelerates
items.summary: Meta has withdrawn from a major clean energy pact as the company intensifies its investments in natural gas infrastructure. This decision follows Meta's significant natural gas buildout over the past year.

items.title: Meta launched a new AI optimism ad set to a song about human extinction
items.summary: Meta released a new AI optimism ad that controversially used David Bowie's song 'Five Years,' which is about impending human extinction. The unusual song choice created a dissonant message for the ad's hopeful theme about AI.

items.title: Midjourney acquired the astrology app Co-Star
items.summary: The AI research lab Midjourney has acquired the astrology app Co-Star, signaling an expansion of its focus beyond image and video generation into new applications. This move diversifies Midjourney's AI portfolio.

items.title: Mobileye CEO Amnon Shashua to step aside as company pushes into robotaxis, robotics
items.summary: Mobileye CEO Amnon Shashua is stepping down from his executive role but will transition to chairman of the board, as the company increasingly focuses on robotaxis and robotics. This leadership change aligns with Mobileye's strategic push into autonomous vehicle and robotic applications.

items.title: OpenAI’s new voice mode makes it to the ChatGPT desktop app
items.summary: OpenAI's new voice mode has been integrated into the ChatGPT desktop app, enabling users to interact with the AI using spoken commands. This feature can work with ChatGPT Work and Codex to complete tasks and control agents.

items.title: Patreon lays off 20% of its workforce
items.summary: Patreon is laying off 20% of its workforce, a decision attributed to market changes and the necessity to adjust its cost structure. Despite the company's core business remaining strong, the move aims to ensure long-term stability.

items.title: Rivian sues the US government for ‘full refund’ of Trump tariffs
items.summary: Carmaker Rivian is suing the U.S. government, seeking a 'full refund' in the 'tens of millions of dollars' for Trump-era tariffs it claims were improperly imposed. This legal action highlights ongoing disputes over trade policies and their financial impact on businesses.

items.title: Runway launches AI model router as generative media gets crowded
items.summary: Runway has introduced its AI model router, Media Router, designed to automatically select the best image, video, or audio generation model for a request. This tool helps developers prioritize quality, speed, or cost in the competitive generative media landscape.

items.title: Tesla’s door handles may spur new US safety rules
items.summary: The new rule-making process follows a series of incidents, including fatal ones, in which people have become stuck inside cars.

items.title: Tesla’s robotaxis are moving in reverse
items.summary: Tesla's robotaxi program saw a 36% decline in paid miles traveled during the second quarter, despite expanding into new cities. This decrease indicates a potential setback in the adoption or performance of its autonomous vehicle services.

items.title: US government says Iran-linked hackers are disrupting American water and energy providers
items.summary: The US government has issued an advisory warning that Iran-linked hackers are actively disrupting American water and energy providers. This updated alert highlights ongoing cybersecurity threats to critical infrastructure.

items.title: History Was Sentenced to Death in My City. It Survived.
items.summary: A historian from Mosul argues that while algorithms and AI threaten to obscure history, human experience and documentation ensure its survival, drawing on personal work documenting the erasure of Jews in his city. The piece responds to a debate about history being defeated by algorithms.

items.title: Sending My Kid to Camp
items.summary: A personal essay reflects on the emotional experience of sending a child to sleepaway camp for the first time, evoking feelings of separation and homesickness. The author notes how these early experiences prepare individuals for similar feelings of newness throughout life.

items.title: TGIF: Kids Doing Fireworks
items.summary: An opinion piece discusses various current events, including the arrest of influencer Andrew Tate on sex trafficking charges, US companies securing Iraq deals to bypass the Strait of Hormuz amidst the US-Iran war, and a software glitch that registered non-citizens to vote in New Jersey. It also offers sarcastic commentary on an anti-ICE protest.

items.title: The Rise of the Navy SEALs, with Ben Milligan
items.summary: Ben Milligan's book 'By Water Beneath the Walls' explores the history and evolution of the US Navy SEALs, tracing their origins from WWII's Underwater Demolition Teams and their development through conflicts. The discussion also touches upon the SEALs' future role in great power competition.

items.title: Why Mamdani Thinks a Millionaire Is Working Class
items.summary: An opinion piece criticizes New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani for his expansive definition of 'working class,' which he suggested could include individuals earning up to a million dollars annually. The author contends this redefinition reflects a 'luxury belief class' and blurs traditional political categories.

items.title: Dostoyevsky in Love
items.summary: Fyodor Dostoyevsky’s personal letters reveal his deep and complex love for Maria, a widowed woman who chose another man after his return from Siberia. Despite his heartbreak, Dostoyevsky selflessly worked to secure her financial well-being.

items.title: Alexa Plus is getting an AI update to handle more complicated instructions
items.summary: Amazon is updating its Alexa Plus assistant with AI capabilities to handle more complex smart home instructions by automatically connecting to and routing requests to various supported devices. This update will allow Alexa to understand nuanced commands for appliances from multiple brands.

items.title: Boox is taking on Xteink with its own tiny e-reader
items.summary: Boox is launching a new series of tiny e-readers, beginning with the Picco, which will feature a 3.97-inch front-lit e-paper display and expandable storage. This move aims to compete with Xteink's popular small-form-factor e-readers at an accessible price point.

items.title: Facebook considers giving up and becoming TikTok
items.summary: Facebook is planning significant changes to its app, including testing a new full-screen video experience upon opening, to better compete with TikTok. This 'reimagined experience' will first roll out in video-preferring countries, with a potential US expansion next year.

items.title: Microsoft pressures LG into killing unwanted McAfee ads
items.summary: Microsoft intervened to stop LG from barraging Windows 11 users with unwanted McAfee trial pop-ups from its LG Monitor App Installer. LG has agreed to immediately disable the McAfee pop-up, with Microsoft promising continued efforts with partners to improve the experience.

items.title: Tesla’s car door defect could lead to tougher rules for everyone
items.summary:
A door handle on a Tesla Model Y at a charging station in San Francisco, California, US, on Monday, Nov. 10, 2025. | David Paul Morris/Bloomberg via Getty Images
Tesla's electronic door handles that have been linked to several deaths could lead to tougher safety rules for the ent…

items.title: TikTok’s protection of minors should not be opt-in, warns EU
items.summary: The European Union has criticized TikTok's protection of minors, stating that privacy settings for children's accounts should not be opt-in under the Digital Services Act. The Commission suggests content posted by minors should be private by default and not recommended in the 'For You' feed.
